name: react-graph-ai
displayName: React Graph AI — Token-Slim AI Context for React
description: Prune your React/Next.js codebase to minimal AI context. Reduces LLM token usage by 90%+ for GitHub Copilot, Claude, ChatGPT, and any AI tool.

chatParticipants.name: reactgraph
chatParticipants.description: Token-optimized context for React/Next.js codebases. Use @reactgraph in chat to query the behavioral graph.

commands.name: context
commands.description: Get pruned context for a component or query. Example: /context UserAuthForm

commands.name: impact
commands.description: Show blast radius of changing a component. Example: /impact Button

commands.name: tree
commands.description: Show render tree for a component. Example: /tree DashboardLayout

languageModelTools.name: react-graph-ai_get_context
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns a token-optimized structural summary AND full source code for the React/Next.js components and utilities most relevant to a query. Includes render relationships, dependents, hooks, and imported utility sources via behavioral graph analysis. Call this BEFORE reading source files or answering questions about React/Next.js code in this workspace — it reduces the token cost by 90-99% vs reading raw files. Takes a 'query' string (component name, file name, or description of what to analyze).

query.description: Component name (e.g. 'UserAuthForm'), file name, or short description of what to analyze (e.g. 'auth form', 'dashboard session check').

languageModelTools.name: react-graph-ai_get_impact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the blast radius of changing a React component — which components re-render, list of dependents, and impact level (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH). Call this BEFORE suggesting changes to any shared component to avoid unintended regressions. Takes a 'componentName' string.

componentName.description: Name of the component you are about to change
